title: Rob Gronkowski and Julian Edelman take part in Tartan Army march at Boston’s World Cup ‘Fan Fest’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: World Cup Gronkowski and Edelman joked that Boston hasn't been drank this dry since Super Bowl 49.
Rob Gronkowski and Julian Edelman landed back in Boston to party with the Scots Friday.

(Lily Hernandez/Fox Sports) As the Scots continue to drink through Boston’s beer supply while celebrating the World Cup this month, a pair of Patriots legends returned to the Commonwealth to take part in the festivities.

title: Scotland vs. Morocco World Cup updates: Ismael Saibari’s early goal gives Morocco an early lead
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: World Cup Stay tuned as the Scots try to stay atop Group C.
Scotland and Morocco walk out prior to the World Cup matchup at "Boston Stadium" (Gillette Stadium).

Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff By Hayden Bird June 19, 2026 | 4:46 PM 2 minutes to read Scotland returns to “Boston Stadium” (Gillette Stadium) for its second World Cup game to face Morocco on Friday night, with another capacity crowd expected.

title: NBA insider: Celtics avoiding this ‘elephant in the room’ during Giannis Antetokounmpo pursuit
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics Jaylen Brown would reportedly need to be a part of any deal involving the Celtics landing Antetokounmpo.

Giannis Antetokounmpo AP Photo/Aaron Gash The Celtics are reportedly on the shortlist of teams to watch in the Giannis Antetokounmpo sweepstakes, and it’s not exactly clear yet just how much Milwaukee would need to get back in return for the two-time league-MVP.
According to The Athletic’s Sam Amick, Boston’s interest in pursuing Antetokounmpo is largely based around a 1-for-1 swap of star players Antetokounmpo and the Celtics’ Jaylen Brown.
